questions.question: This primetime soap about twenty-somethings living in an apartment complex, starred Heather Locklear and Andrew Shue.
By using short-range UHF radio waves, what now-common technology was first offered to consumers in 2001 offering cordless connectivity?
What Tulsa, Oklahoma, band had a hit song in 1997 called 'MMMBop'?
Who was the 1996 PGA Rookie of the Year?
Especially during competition, the exclamation 'Booyah!' was typically followed by what phrase?
